name: Chile Salsa (Hot Tomato-Pepper Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
Yield: 6 to 8 pints
Procedure:
1. Wear rubber gloves while handling chilies or wash hands thoroughly with soap and water before touching your face.
2. Peel and prepare chili peppers as described in making Mexican Tomato Sauce.
3. Wash tomatoes and dip in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds, or until skins split.
4. Dip in cold water, slip off the skins, and remove cores.
5. Coarsely chop tomatoes and combine with chopped peppers, onions, and remaining ingredients in a large saucepan.
6. Heat to boil, then simmer for 10 minutes.
7. Fill jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
8. Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Chile Salsa in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 minutes
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 25 minutes
